revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kimitezuri]] <includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of Kimitezuri]]</includeonly> <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>
海と太陽を司る琉球王国の守護神である。琉球王国の存亡の危機に降臨すると言われている。ニライカナイ（海の深淵、あるいは天上）に住まうと言われている。新しい国王が、即位の儀式中、聞得大君に憑依すると言われている。

'''君手摩'''（きみてずり）は、海と太陽を司る[[琉球王国]]の守護神である。琉球王国の存亡の危機に降臨すると言われている。[[ニライカナイ]]（海の深淵、あるいは天上、海の彼方にある）に住まうと言われている。新しい国王が、即位の儀式中、[[聞得大君]]に憑依すると言われている。
== 関連 == * [[沖縄県関連の記事一覧]] * [[沖縄県]] * [[神]]・[[神話]]
{{stub}} [[category:沖縄の宗教|きみてすり]] [[category:日本の神|きみてすり]]

'''君手摩'''（きみてずり）は、海と太陽を司る[[琉球王国]]の守護神である。琉球王国の存亡の危機に降臨すると言われている。[[ニライカナイ]]（海の深淵、あるいは天上、海の彼方にある）に住まうと言われている。新しい国王が、即位の儀式中、[[聞得大君]]に憑依すると言われている。
== 関連 == * [[沖縄県関連の記事一覧]] * [[沖縄県]] * [[神]]・[[神話]]
{{stub}} [[category:沖縄の宗教|きみてすり]] [[category:日本の神|きみてすり]] [[category:太陽神|きみてすり]] [[category:海神|きみてすり]]

'''君手摩'''（きみてずり）は、海と太陽を司る[[琉球王国]]の守護神である。琉球王国の存亡の機に降臨すると言われている。[[ニライカナイ]]（海の深淵、あるいは天上、海の彼方にある）に住まうと言われている。新しい国王が、即位の儀式中、[[聞得大君]]に憑依すると言われている。
== 関連 == * [[沖縄県関連の記事一覧]] * [[沖縄県]] * [[神]]・[[神話]]
{{stub}} [[category:沖縄の宗教|きみてすり]] [[category:日本の神|きみてすり]] [[category:太陽神|きみてすり]] [[category:海神|きみてすり]]

'''君手摩'''（きみてずり）は、通説では海と太陽を司る[[琉球王国]]の守護神とされる。琉球王国の存亡の機に降臨すると言われる。[[ニライカナイ]]（海の深淵、あるいは天上、海の彼方、地下にある）に住まうとされており、新しい国王の即位の儀式中、[[聞得大君]]に憑依するとされる。
ただし、君手摩を神の名とする初出史料は尚象賢（[[羽地朝秀]]）が編纂した[[中山世鑑]]であるが、「君」は琉球の祭祀をつかさどる祝女（ノロ）を意味しており、「手摩」は祈祷の際に手をすり合わせることを意味すること、また君手摩を行事として記載する史料もあることから、神名ではなく、宗教儀式名であると解釈する説もある。

君手摩を神の名とする初出史料は尚象賢（[[羽地朝秀]]）が編纂した[[中山世鑑]]である。 しかし、一般に「君」は琉球の祭祀をつかさどる祝女（ノロ）を意味し、「手摩」は祈祷の際に手をすり合わせることを意味すること、また君手摩を行事として記載する別の史料もあることから、君手摩は神名ではなく、宗教儀式名であると解釈する説もある。

revision.comment: r2.5.2) (ロボットによる 追加: [[zh:君手摩]]
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2010年10月}} '''君手摩'''（きみてずり）または'''君手'''（きみて）、'''君てっ'''（きみてっ、きみって）は、通説では[[海]]と[[太陽]]を司る[[琉球王国]]の[[守護神]]とされており、琉球王国の存亡の機に降臨すると言われる。[[ニライカナイ]]（海の深淵、海の彼方、地下にある）に住まうとされており、新しい国王の即位の儀式中、[[聞得大君]]に憑依するとされる。
君手摩を神の名とする初出史料は向象賢（[[羽地朝秀]]）が編纂した『[[中山世鑑]]』である。しかし、一般に「君」は琉球の祭祀をつかさどる祝女（[[ノロ]]）を意味し、「手摩」は祈祷の際に手をすり合わせることを意味すること、また君手摩を行事として記載する別の史料もあることから、君手摩は神名ではなく、宗教儀式名であると解釈する説もある。
== 関連 == * [[琉球の信仰]] * [[神]]・[[神話]]
{{Myth-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きみてすり}} [[Category:琉球の信仰]] [[Category:琉球の神]] [[Category:太陽神]] [[Category:海神]]
[[zh:君手摩]]

{{出典の明記|date=2010年10月}} '''君手摩'''（きみてずり）または'''君手'''（きみて）、'''君てっ'''（きみてっ、きみって）は、通説では[[海]]と[[太陽]]を司る[[琉球王国]]の[[守護神]]とされており、琉球王国の存亡の機に降臨すると言われる。[[ニライカナイ]]（海の深淵、海の彼方、地下にある）に住まうとされており、新しい国王の即位の儀式中、[[聞得大君]]に憑依するとされる。
君手摩を神の名とする初出史料は向象賢（[[羽地朝秀]]）が編纂した『[[中山世鑑]]』である。しかし、一般に「君」は琉球の祭祀をつかさどる祝女（[[ノロ]]）を意味し、「手摩」は祈祷の際に手をすり合わせることを意味すること、また君手摩を行事として記載する別の史料もあることから、君手摩は神名ではなく、宗教儀式名であると解釈する説もある。

{{出典の明記|date=2010年10月}} '''君手摩'''（きみてずり）または'''君手'''（きみて）、'''君てっ'''（きみてっ、きみって）は、[[琉球神道]]に伝わる女神。通説では[[海]]と[[太陽]]を司る[[琉球王国]]の[[守護神]]とされており、琉球王国の存亡の機に降臨すると言われる。[[ニライカナイ]]（海の深淵、海の彼方、地下にある）に住まうとされており、新しい国王の即位の儀式中、[[聞得大君]]に憑依するとされる。古くから[[キンマモン]]と同一視されてきた。
君手摩を神の名とする初出史料は向象賢（[[羽地朝秀]]）が編纂した『[[中山世鑑]]』である。しかし、一般に「君」は琉球の祭祀をつかさどる祝女（[[ノロ]]）を意味し、「手摩」は祈祷の際に手をすり合わせることを意味すること、また君手摩を行事として記載する別の史料もあることから、君手摩は神名ではなく、宗教儀式名であると解釈する説もある。
==参考文献== *戸部民夫『「日本の女神様」がよくわかる本』[[PHP研究所]]、2007年。

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:君手摩]][[zh:君手摩]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10918482]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q10918482]]
Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kimitezuri''' (君手摩, also known as '''Kimite''' (君手) or '''Kimitecchi''' (君てっ, きみって)) is a goddess in [[:en:Ryukyuan religion|Ryukyuan Shinto]]. According to tradition, she is regarded as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], presiding over the [[:en:Sea|sea]] and the [[:en:Sun|sun]]. It is believed that she descends at moments of crisis for the kingdom. She is said to reside in {{ill|Nirai Kanai|ja|ニライカナイ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirai Kanai|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nirai Kanai|WD=Q24885856}}, a mystical realm described as being in the depths of the sea, beyond the horizon, or underground. During the enthronement ceremony of a new ruler, she is believed to possess the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]], the highest-Ranking priestess of the kingdom. Historically, she has been closely associated with {{ill|Kinmamun|ja|キンマモン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmamun|qq=|1=kinmanmontomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867498}}.

The earliest historical record of Kimitezuri as a deity appears in the ''[[:en:Chūzan Seikan|Chūzan Seikan]]'', compiled by {{ill|Shō Genken|ja|向象賢|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Genken|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 羽地朝秀|1=Shō Shōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843010}} (also known as [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]]). However, some scholars argue that "Kimitezuri" may not refer to a specific deity but rather a religious ritual. The word "Kimite" (君) traditionally refers to a [[:en:Noro (priestess)|Noro]], a female shaman responsible for religious ceremonies in Ryukyuan worship, while "Tesuri" (手摩) means rubbing hands together in prayer. Other historical records also describe Kimitezuri as a ceremonial practice rather than a divine figure.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Bibliography ==
* {{ill|Tamio Tobe|ja|戸部民夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamio Tobe|qq=|1=Tamio Tobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277399}}, ''Understanding the Goddesses of Japan'' (『「日本の女神様」がよくわかる本』), PHP Institute, 2007.

== See Also ==
* [[:en:Ryukyuan religion|Ryukyuan Shinto]] - {{ill|Faith in Ryukyu|ja|琉球における信仰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faith in Ryukyu|qq=|1=religion in Ryukyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97188899}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10918482]]

Wikidata name: (none)
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kimitezuri''' (君手摩, also known as '''Kimite''' (君手) or '''Kimitecchi''' (君てっ, きみって)) is a goddess in [[:en:Ryukyuan religion|Ryukyuan Shinto]]. According to tradition, she is regarded as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], presiding over the [[:en:Sea|sea]] and the [[:en:Sun|sun]]. It is believed that she descends at moments of crisis for the kingdom. She is said to reside in {{ill|Nirai Kanai|ja|ニライカナイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirai Kanai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nirai Kanai|WD=Q24885856}}, a mystical realm described as being in the depths of the sea, beyond the horizon, or underground. During the enthronement ceremony of a new ruler, she is believed to possess the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]], the highest-Ranking priestess of the kingdom. Historically, she has been closely associated with {{ill|Kinmamun|ja|キンマモン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmamun|1=kinmanmontomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867498}}.

The earliest historical record of Kimitezuri as a deity appears in the ''[[:en:Chūzan Seikan|Chūzan Seikan]]'', compiled by {{ill|Shō Genken|ja|向象賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Genken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 羽地朝秀|1=Shō Shōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843010}} (also known as [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]]). However, some scholars argue that "Kimitezuri" may not refer to a specific deity but rather a religious ritual. The word "Kimite" (君) traditionally refers to a [[:en:Noro (priestess)|Noro]], a female shaman responsible for religious ceremonies in Ryukyuan worship, while "Tesuri" (手摩) means rubbing hands together in prayer. Other historical records also describe Kimitezuri as a ceremonial practice rather than a divine figure.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:君手摩]][[zh:君手摩]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10918482]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10918482]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kimitezuri''' (君手摩, also known as '''Kimite''' (君手) or '''Kimitecchi''' (君てっ, きみって)) is a goddess in [[:en:Ryukyuan religion|Ryukyuan Shinto]]. According to tradition, she is regarded as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], presiding over the [[:en:Sea|sea]] and the [[:en:Sun|sun]]. It is believed that she descends at moments of crisis for the kingdom. She is said to reside in {{ill|Nirai Kanai|ja|ニライカナイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirai Kanai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nirai Kanai|WD=Q24885856}}, a mystical realm described as being in the depths of the sea, beyond the horizon, or underground. During the enthronement ceremony of a new ruler, she is believed to possess the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]], the highest-Ranking priestess of the kingdom. Historically, she has been closely associated with {{ill|Kinmamun|ja|キンマモン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmamun|1=kinmanmontomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867498}}.
The earliest historical record of Kimitezuri as a deity appears in the ''[[:en:Chūzan Seikan|Chūzan Seikan]]'', compiled by {{ill|Shō Genken|ja|向象賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Genken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 羽地朝秀|1=Shō Shōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843010}} (also known as [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]]). However, some scholars argue that "Kimitezuri" may not refer to a specific deity but rather a religious ritual. The word "Kimite" (君) traditionally refers to a [[:en:Noro (priestess)|Noro]], a female shaman responsible for religious ceremonies in Ryukyuan worship, while "Tesuri" (手摩) means rubbing hands together in prayer. Other historical records also describe Kimitezuri as a ceremonial practice rather than a divine figure.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Tamio Tobe|ja|戸部民夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamio Tobe|1=Tamio Tobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277399}}, ''Understanding the Goddesses of Japan'' (『「日本の女神様」がよくわかる本』), PHP Institute, 2007.
== See Also == * [[:en:Ryukyuan religion|Ryukyuan Shinto]] - {{ill|Faith in Ryukyu|ja|琉球における信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faith in Ryukyu|1=religion in Ryukyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97188899}} * [[:en:Deity|Kami]]・[[:en:Myth|Mythology]]

It is believed that she descends at moments of crisis for the kingdom. '''Kimitezuri''' (君手摩, also known as '''Kimite''' (君手) or '''Kimitecchi''' (君てっ, きみって)) is a goddess in [[:en:Ryukyuan religion|Ryukyuan Shinto]]. According to tradition, she is regarded as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], presiding over the [[:en:Sea|sea]] and the [[:en:Sun|sun]]. It is believed that she descends at moments of crisis for the kingdom. She is said to reside in {{ill|Nirai Kanai|ja|ニライカナイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirai Kanai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nirai Kanai|WD=Q24885856}}, a mystical realm described as being in the depths of the sea, beyond the horizon, or underground. During the enthronement ceremony of a new ruler, she is believed to possess the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]], the highest-Ranking priestess of the kingdom. Historically, she has been closely associated with {{ill|Kinmamun|ja|キンマモン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmamun|1=kinmanmontomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867498}}.

The earliest historical record of Kimitezuri as a deity appears in the ''[[:en:Chūzan Seikan|Chūzan Seikan]]'', compiled by {{ill|Shō Genken|ja|向象賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Genken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 羽地朝秀|1=Shō Shōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843010}} (also known as [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]]). However, some scholars argue that "Kimitezuri" may not refer to a specific deity but rather a religious ritual. The word "Kimite" (君) traditionally refers to a [[:en:Noro (priestess)|Noro]], a female shaman responsible for religious ceremonies in Ryukyuan worship, while "Tesuri" (手摩) means rubbing hands together in prayer. Other historical records also describe Kimitezuri as a ceremonial practice rather than a divine figure.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:君手摩]][[zh:君手摩]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:君手摩]][[zh:君手摩]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:君手摩]][[zh:君手摩]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10918482]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10918482]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kimitezuri''' (君手摩, also known as '''Kimite''' (君手) or '''Kimitecchi''' (君てっ, きみって)) is a goddess in [[:en:Ryukyuan religion|Ryukyuan Shinto]]. According to tradition, she is regarded as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the [[:en:Ryukyu Kingdom|Ryukyu Kingdom]], presiding over the [[:en:Sea|sea]] and the [[:en:Sun|sun]]. It is believed that she descends at moments of crisis for the kingdom. She is said to reside in {{ill|Nirai Kanai|ja|ニライカナイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirai Kanai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nirai Kanai|WD=Q24885856}}, a mystical realm described as being in the depths of the sea, beyond the horizon, or underground. During the enthronement ceremony of a new ruler, she is believed to possess the [[:en:Kikoe-ōgimi|Kikoe-ōgimi]], the highest-Ranking priestess of the kingdom. Historically, she has been closely associated with {{ill|Kinmamun|ja|キンマモン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmamun|1=kinmanmontomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867498}}.
The earliest historical record of Kimitezuri as a deity appears in the ''[[:en:Chūzan Seikan|Chūzan Seikan]]'', compiled by {{ill|Shō Genken|ja|向象賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Genken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 羽地朝秀|1=Shō Shōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843010}} (also known as [[:en:Shō Shōken|Haneji Chōshū]]). However, some scholars argue that "Kimitezuri" may not refer to a specific deity but rather a religious ritual. The word "Kimite" (君) traditionally refers to a [[:en:Noro (priestess)|Noro]], a female shaman responsible for religious ceremonies in Ryukyuan worship, while "Tesuri" (手摩) means rubbing hands together in prayer. Other historical records also describe Kimitezuri as a ceremonial practice rather than a divine figure.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Tamio Tobe|ja|戸部民夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamio Tobe|1=Tamio Tobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277399}}, ''Understanding the Goddesses of Japan'' (『「日本の女神様」がよくわかる本』), PHP Institute, 2007.
== See Also == * [[:en:Ryukyuan religion|Ryukyuan Shinto]] - {{ill|Faith in Ryukyu|ja|琉球における信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faith in Ryukyu|1=religion in Ryukyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97188899}} * [[:en:Deity|Kami]]・[[:en:Myth|Mythology]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:君手摩]][[zh:君手摩]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:君手摩]][[zh:君手摩]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:君手摩]][[zh:君手摩]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10918482]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q10918482]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kimitezuri''' (君手摩, also known as '''Kimite''' (君手) or '''Kimitecchi''' (君てっ, きみって)) is a goddess in {{ill|Ryukyuan religion|en|Ryukyuan religion|WD=Q892785|lt=Ryukyuan Shinto}}. According to tradition, she is regarded as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}}, presiding over the {{ill|Sea|en|Sea|WD=Q165|lt=sea}} and the {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|WD=Q525|lt=sun}}. '''Kimitezuri''' (君手摩, also known as '''Kimite''' (君手) or '''Kimitecchi''' (君てっ, きみって)) is a goddess in {{ill|Ryukyuan religion|en|Ryukyuan religion|WD=Q892785|lt=Ryukyuan Shinto}}. According to tradition, she is regarded as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}}, presiding over the {{ill|Sea|en|Sea|WD=Q165|lt=sea}} and the {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|WD=Q525|lt=sun}}. It is believed that she descends at moments of crisis for the kingdom. She is said to reside in {{ill|Nirai Kanai|ja|ニライカナイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirai Kanai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nirai Kanai|WD=Q24885856}}, a mystical realm described as being in the depths of the sea, beyond the horizon, or underground. During the enthronement ceremony of a new ruler, she is believed to possess the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|en|Kikoe-ōgimi|WD=Q11611193|lt=Kikoe-ōgimi}}, the highest-Ranking priestess of the kingdom. Historically, she has been closely associated with {{ill|Kinmamun|ja|キンマモン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmamun|1=kinmanmontomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867498}}.

The word "Kimite" (君) traditionally refers to a {{ill|Noro (priestess)|en|Noro (priestess)|WD=Q11325791|lt=Noro}}, a female shaman responsible for religious ceremonies in Ryukyuan worship, while "Tesuri" (手摩) means rubbing hands together in prayer. '''Kimitezuri''' (君手摩, also known as '''Kimite''' (君手) or '''Kimitecchi''' (君てっ, きみって)) is a goddess in {{ill|Ryukyuan religion|en|Ryukyuan religion|WD=Q892785|lt=Ryukyuan Shinto}}. According to tradition, she is regarded as the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the {{ill|Ryukyu Kingdom|en|Ryukyu Kingdom|WD=Q28025|lt=Ryukyu Kingdom}}, presiding over the {{ill|Sea|en|Sea|WD=Q165|lt=sea}} and the {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|WD=Q525|lt=sun}}. It is believed that she descends at moments of crisis for the kingdom. She is said to reside in {{ill|Nirai Kanai|ja|ニライカナイ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nirai Kanai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Nirai Kanai|WD=Q24885856}}, a mystical realm described as being in the depths of the sea, beyond the horizon, or underground. During the enthronement ceremony of a new ruler, she is believed to possess the {{ill|Kikoe-ōgimi|en|Kikoe-ōgimi|WD=Q11611193|lt=Kikoe-ōgimi}}, the highest-Ranking priestess of the kingdom. Historically, she has been closely associated with {{ill|Kinmamun|ja|キンマモン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinmamun|1=kinmanmontomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24867498}}.
The earliest historical record of Kimitezuri as a deity appears in the ''{{ill|Chūzan Seikan|en|Chūzan Seikan|WD=Q8015936|lt=Chūzan Seikan}}'', compiled by {{ill|Shō Genken|ja|向象賢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shō Genken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 羽地朝秀|1=Shō Shōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q843010}} (also known as {{ill|Shō Shōken|en|Shō Shōken|WD=Q843010|lt=Haneji Chōshū}}). However, some scholars argue that "Kimitezuri" may not refer to a specific deity but rather a religious ritual. The word "Kimite" (君) traditionally refers to a {{ill|Noro (priestess)|en|Noro (priestess)|WD=Q11325791|lt=Noro}}, a female shaman responsible for religious ceremonies in Ryukyuan worship, while "Tesuri" (手摩) means rubbing hands together in prayer. Other historical records also describe Kimitezuri as a ceremonial practice rather than a divine figure.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Tamio Tobe|ja|戸部民夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamio Tobe|1=Tamio Tobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277399}}, ''Understanding the Goddesses of Japan'' (『「日本の女神様」がよくわかる本』), PHP Institute, 2007.
== See Also == * {{ill|Ryukyuan religion|en|Ryukyuan religion|WD=Q892785|lt=Ryukyuan Shinto}} - {{ill|Faith in Ryukyu|ja|琉球における信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Faith in Ryukyu|1=religion in Ryukyu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97188899}} * {{ill|Deity|en|Deity|WD=Q178885|lt=Kami}}・{{ill|Myth|en|Myth|WD=Q12827256|lt=Mythology}}
{{Myth-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Okinawa Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|君手摩|version=103360107|comment=Imported full ja history}}
